name: Fetal Bovine Serum
animal_source_tissue: Blood cardiac puncture post-slaughter of dam
back_calc_method: DIRECT — Pecora et al 2020 industry average 0.4L serum per fetal calf
notes: Industry average 0.4L serum per fetal calf = ~2.5 calves/L. FBS is NOT a by-product — dam selected for pregnancy.
source_citation: Pecora et al 2020 Translational Animal Science DOI:10.1093/tas/txag044
animal_use_category: KILLED — fetal dam killed at slaughter; fetus cardiac puncture

name: Calf Bovine Serum
animal_source_tissue: Blood venipuncture from living calves — animals NOT killed
back_calc_method: DIRECT — ATCC 302030 product description confirms venipuncture from living calves
notes: *ATCC 302030 iron-fortified version: blood collected from donor calves by venipuncture ages 16-22 weeks. Animals are NOT killed for serum collection. Marked NO† — animals used but not killed. Impact = 0 in calculator but welfare concern noted.
source_citation: ATCC 302030 product page screenshot verified 2026-06-24; Fisher/Encompass catalog 50-238-4262
animal_use_category: USED-NOT-KILLED† — calves bled by venipuncture ages 16-22 weeks per ATCC 302030; not slaughtered for serum

name: Horse Serum
animal_source_tissue: Blood repeated bleeds from live donor herd
back_calc_method: DIRECT — ATCC Animal Cell Culture Guide confirms horses not killed
notes: Animals NOT killed. Closed donor herd per ATCC guide. Marked NO† — animals used but not killed. Impact = 0 in calculator but welfare concern noted.
source_citation: ATCC Animal Cell Culture Guide verified 2026-06-24
animal_use_category: USED-NOT-KILLED† — horses maintained as closed donor herd; repeated bleeds over lifetime; not killed

name: Bovine Trypsin
back_calc_method: BACK-CALC: pancreas 400g x 2% trypsin x 80% protein fraction x 50% extraction = 3.2g/animal; 0.25% sol = 2.5g/L; animals/L = 2.5/3.2 = 0.78
notes: *DERIVED ESTIMATE — see back_calculation_methodology.md. Slaughterhouse by-product — animals NOT killed specifically for trypsin.
source_citation: Derivation from bovine pancreas anatomy and trypsin content. Worthington Biochemical documentation.
animal_use_category: KILLED-BYPRODUCT — bovine pancreas from slaughterhouse; not killed specifically for trypsin

name: Matrigel
synonyms: Basement Membrane Extract;BME;Growth Factor Reduced Matrigel
back_calc_method: BACK-CALC (model): EHS tumor 6g x 4% extractable BM protein = 240mg/mouse; Matrigel 10mg/mL => 24mL/mouse; animals/mL = 1/24 = 0.042. See back_calculation_methodology.md and matrigel_model.py
notes: *HIGH-UNCERTAINTY Tier-2 estimate. Range 0.013-0.20 animals/mL, driven mainly by the extractable BM-protein fraction (2-6%). Use for order-of-magnitude comparison, not exact counts. Xeno-free alternatives (Vitronectin XF, recombinant laminin-521) avoid the question.
source_citation: Kleinman 2001 Curr Protoc Cell Biol DOI:10.1002/0471143030.cb1002s00; Kleinman et al 1982 Biochemistry DOI:10.1021/bi00350a005; composition Kastana et al 2019; conc Corning Matrigel CoA. Derived model - see back_calculation_methodology.md
animal_use_category: KILLED — EHS mouse sarcoma; mice killed for tumor harvest

name: Bovine Collagen Type I
back_calc_method: BACK-CALC: hide 27kg x 30% collagen x 40% extraction = 3240g/animal; animals/kg = 1000/3240 = 0.31
notes: *DERIVED ESTIMATE. Slaughterhouse by-product. Near-zero per liter — expressed per kg.
source_citation: Derivation from bovine hide mass and collagen content. Advanced BioMatrix PureCol documentation.
animal_use_category: KILLED-BYPRODUCT — bovine hide from slaughterhouse; not killed specifically for collagen

name: Bovine Serum Albumin
back_calc_method: BACK-CALC: 30L blood x 50% serum = 15L; albumin 35g/L x 15L = 525g; 35% recovery = 184g/animal; animals/kg = 1000/184 = 5.4
source_citation: Derivation from bovine blood volume and albumin concentration. Sigma-Aldrich A7906 A1470 verified 2026-06-24.
animal_use_category: KILLED-BYPRODUCT — bovine serum from slaughterhouse; not killed specifically for BSA

name: Bovine Insulin
back_calc_method: BACK-CALC: pancreas 400g x 200ug/g insulin x 60% extraction = 48mg/animal; animals/g = 1000mg/48mg = 20.8
notes: *DERIVED ESTIMATE. NOTE: most cell culture insulin is now recombinant human (0 animals) — verify before logging.
source_citation: Derivation from bovine pancreatic insulin content. Steiner et al Diabetes 1972.
animal_use_category: KILLED-BYPRODUCT — bovine pancreas from slaughterhouse; not killed specifically for insulin

name: Murine Monoclonal Antibodies
back_calc_method: BACK-CALC: not applicable — ascites volume per mouse variable 1-10mL
notes: *DERIVED ESTIMATE. NOTE: most commercial mAbs now in-vitro produced (0 animals). Verify with supplier.
source_citation: Harlow and Lane Antibodies A Laboratory Manual CSHL Press.
animal_use_category: KILLED — mice killed for ascites harvest; verify if in-vitro produced

name: Laminin Murine
back_calc_method: BACK-CALC (model): same EHS source as R005; 240mg matrix x 60% laminin x 50% purification = 72mg laminin/mouse; product 1mg/mL => 72mL/mouse; animals/mL = 1/72 = 0.014. See back_calculation_methodology.md
notes: *HIGH-UNCERTAINTY Tier-2 estimate. Range 0.0056-0.11 animals/mL. Purified laminin is sold dilute (~1mg/mL) so per-mL impact is lower than Matrigel. Recombinant laminin-521 (BioLamina) is a 0-animal drop-in.
source_citation: Kleinman 2001 Curr Protoc Cell Biol DOI:10.1002/0471143030.cb1002s00; composition Kastana et al 2019; laminin product specs Sigma/Corning. Derived model - see back_calculation_methodology.md
animal_use_category: KILLED — EHS mouse sarcoma; same source as Matrigel

name: Fibronectin Bovine
back_calc_method: BACK-CALC: 15L plasma x 300ug/mL fibronectin x 50% recovery = 2.25g/animal; animals/g = 1/2.25 = 0.44
source_citation: Derivation from Mosesson and Amrani J Clin Invest 1980.
animal_use_category: KILLED-BYPRODUCT — bovine plasma from slaughterhouse; by-product

name: DMEM
synonyms: Dulbeccos Modified Eagle Medium;high glucose DMEM

name: Penicillin-Streptomycin
notes: Penicillin fungal-derived; streptomycin bacterial-derived. No animal use.

name: Collagenase Type II
notes: Bacterial enzyme — no animal origin. Used to digest tissue during primary cell isolation but enzyme itself = 0 animals.
source_citation: Worthington Biochemical Collagenase Type II; Zhou et al 2022 DOI:10.1038/s41392-022-01044-5

name: Porcine Trypsin
synonyms: Trypsin-EDTA porcine;0.25% Trypsin porcine;irradiated porcine trypsin;Gibco 25200056
back_calc_method: BACK-CALC: porcine pancreas ~150-300g per pig; trypsin content ~1-3% dry weight; extraction efficiency ~40-60%; 0.25% solution = 2.5g/L; mid estimate ~0.6 animals/L
notes: *DERIVED ESTIMATE — same methodology as R004 but porcine anatomy. Slaughterhouse by-product. Gibco 25200056 is porcine not bovine — verify which your protocol uses.
source_citation: Derivation from porcine pancreas anatomy. Thermo Fisher product page 25200056 confirming porcine origin verified 2026-06-24.
animal_use_category: KILLED-BYPRODUCT — porcine pancreas from slaughterhouse; not killed specifically for trypsin

name: Rat Tail Collagen Type I
synonyms: Collagen I rat tail;rat tail tendon collagen;Corning 354249
animal_source_tissue: Tail tendon — dedicated kill not slaughterhouse by-product
back_calc_method: BACK-CALC: rat tail tendon mass ~0.5-1g per rat; collagen content ~70% dry weight; extraction efficiency ~30-50%; cell culture solution ~3mg/mL = 3g/L; animals/L = 3/(0.75 x 0.7 x 0.4) = ~14
notes: *DERIVED ESTIMATE. IMPORTANT: rats killed specifically for tail tendon harvest — dedicated kill not by-product. Higher animals/liter than bovine collagen by ~30-50x.
source_citation: Derived from rat anatomy. Corning 354249 product page confirming rat tail tendon source verified 2026-06-24.
animal_use_category: KILLED — rats killed specifically for tail tendon harvest; dedicated kill not byproduct
